wine_festivals.name: Chianti Classico Collection
wine_festivals.recurrence_pattern: annual, second or third week of February
wine_festivals.description: More than 200 Chianti Classico producers pour the new annata, Riserva and Gran Selezione vintages from over 680 labels at Stazione Leopolda in Florence. The opening day is trade-only, the second opens to consumers.
wine_festivals.tip: Tickets release late January; the consortium prioritises trade and press on day one and consumers on day two.

wine_festivals.name: Anteprima della Vernaccia di San Gimignano
wine_festivals.host_organization: Consorzio della Vernaccia di San Gimignano
wine_festivals.description: The Consorzio della Vernaccia presents the new vintage of Tuscany sole DOCG white inside the Museo Civico in San Gimignano, with 40-plus producers pouring annata and Riserva.
wine_festivals.tip: The historical centre of San Gimignano fills quickly on tasting day; reserve a stay in Pancole or the Castagno area five minutes out.

wine_festivals.name: Anteprima del Vino Nobile di Montepulciano
wine_festivals.host_organization: Consorzio del Vino Nobile di Montepulciano
wine_festivals.description: A record 57 producers pour Vino Nobile annata, Riserva and the newly introduced Pieve sub-zone tier inside the Fortezza di Montepulciano. Two press days precede the public tasting and assign vintage stars.
wine_festivals.tip: Combine the Anteprima with a walk through the Cantina Contucci and Cantina De Ricci, both carved into the medieval cellars beneath the town hall.

wine_festivals.name: Benvenuto Brunello
wine_festivals.recurrence_pattern: annual, third or fourth week of November
wine_festivals.host_organization: Consorzio del Vino Brunello di Montalcino
wine_festivals.description: Tasting of the freshly released Brunello (five-year-aged) and Brunello Riserva (six-year-aged) inside the cloisters of Sant'Agostino and the Montalcino fortress, with a vintage-rating ceremony assigning a star count from one to five. Since 2018 the consortium holds Benvenuto Brunello in November (previously late February).
wine_festivals.tip: Plan three days minimum: the consortium walk-around, an estate cellar visit, and a vertical lunch at one of the historic Montalcino enoteche.

wine_festivals.name: Cantine Aperte
wine_festivals.description: Around a thousand Italian estates and roughly two hundred Tuscan members of Movimento Turismo del Vino open their cellars to visitors with vineyard hikes, paired meals and harvest-machinery demos. The 2026 edition runs 30 and 31 May.
wine_festivals.tip: Most cellars accept walk-ins but star Brunello and Bolgheri estates require advance booking through the Movimento Turismo del Vino regional site.

wine_festivals.name: Calici di Stelle
wine_festivals.recurrence_pattern: annual, late July to mid August (centred on 10 August)
wine_festivals.duration: approximately 4 weeks of events around the 10 August night
wine_festivals.description: Vineyard tastings paired with stargazing under the Perseid meteor shower on the Night of San Lorenzo. Member estates host open-air dinners, telescopes and music; the 2025 program spanned 25 July to 24 August across more than fifty Tuscan venues.
wine_festivals.tip: Vinci, Montalcino and Castelfalfi run the most production-heavy programs; check the Movimento Turismo del Vino events calendar in early July.

wine_festivals.name: Bolgheri DiVino
wine_festivals.host_organization: Consorzio per la tutela dei vini Bolgheri e Bolgheri Sassicaia DOC
wine_festivals.description: The Consorzio Bolgheri walk-around tasting at the Castello della Gherardesca and the Frantoio of Casa Carducci in Castagneto Carducci, followed by the kilometre-long Cena dei Mille on the cypress-lined Viale dei Cipressi.
wine_festivals.tip: The Cena dei Mille on the Viale dei Cipressi seats one thousand and books out months in advance; the walk-around tasting is easier to secure.

wine_festivals.name: Vino al Vino, Panzano in Chianti
wine_festivals.host_organization: Unione Viticoltori di Panzano in Chianti
wine_festivals.description: Twenty-three producers of the Unione Viticoltori di Panzano pour Chianti Classico Panzano UGA bottlings, Conca d'Oro single-village reds and IGT Toscana from stalls around Piazza Bucciarelli. Over 80 wines are typically open across the weekend.
wine_festivals.tip: The Conca d'Oro sub-zone within Panzano produces some of Chianti Classico's longest-lived bottlings; ask producers to compare a Riserva to a Gran Selezione side by side.
